Maine · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian Guide

Issued by the Board of Licensing of Dietetic Practice under the Maine Office of Professional and Occupational Regulation (OPOR). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$171
$150 license fee + $21 criminal history records check fee
Renewal
$150
Annual (by Dec 31st) + 15 CE hours
Processing Time
3 to 5 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
1 Year
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Credential Categories

Maine Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian

The standard credential to practice dietetics in Maine, overseen by the Board of Licensing of Dietetic Practice.

Temporary Licensed Dietitian

Maine offers a temporary license for applicants awaiting the CDR examination. It requires a supervised practice plan.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Maine Licensed Dietitian

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an accredited dietitian internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status satisfies this.

Submit the Application via Maine OPOR

Create an account on the Maine OPOR online portal, complete the initial application, and pay the required fees ($150 license + $21 background check).

Provide Verification Documents

Submit a copy of your active CDR card or ensure verification is sent directly. A criminal history records check is conducted automatically with the submission of your application and fee.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • CDR Registration CardProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Criminal History Records CheckProcessed internally by the Board upon receipt of the $21 fee with an application.
  • Official College TranscriptsMay be required if not submitting primary source verification via the CDR.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Maine offers a temporary dietitian license for applicants who have met the educational and experience requirements but have not yet passed the examination. Temporary licensees must practice under the supervision of a Maine Licensed Dietitian.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Maine provides licensure by endorsement for applicants holding a substantially equivalent license in another state or holding active registration as a Registered Dietitian with the CDR. A verification of licensure from the other state(s) must be provided.

Frequently Asked Questions

Maine Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Maine dietitian license cost?

The initial cost is $171, which includes a $150 license fee and a $21 criminal history records check fee.

When does the Maine license need to be renewed?

Maine dietitian licenses expire annually on December 31st. The renewal fee is $150. A $50 late fee is added if renewed after the expiration date.

What are the continuing education requirements for Maine?

Licensees must complete 15 hours of approved continuing education annually. At least 4 of these hours must be from approved Category 1 programs.
